aç gidiyorlar, aç. "Aç gitmiyorlar." diyen varsa hodri meydan, beraber gidelim bakalım, bu ülkenin kuruluşları var.
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - Bakalım, gidelim bakalım.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Sayın Bakana teklif ediliyor, 235 milyar lira. Bu arada sizde -çok özür dilerim- 2 Adalet ve Kalkınma Partisi var; biri sürekli vadediyor, öbürü yapmıyor, sizin şeyinizde var. Diyorsunuz ki, yazmışsınız -Sayın Cumhurbaşkanının da resmi var- diyor ki: "Biz bu kadar kişiye veriyoruz, yaygınlaştıracağız." Sayın Bakan, Bakan oldu, ilk iş Mahmut Özer'in okul öncesi okul yemeğini iptal etti. Şimdi de sordular "Niye vermiyorsunuz?" diye "Vallahi bu iş akademik olarak baktık, olmuyor. Yemeği yapacak personelimiz yok. Personel olsa yemek verecek yerimiz yok. Versek de -ben özel okullara sordum- çocuklar yemiyormuş. Çocuklar yemez zaten." dedi. Ya, aç diyorum çocuklar.
YAŞAR KIRKPINAR (İzmir) - Çok güzel...
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Çok güzel resim değil mi? Bu sizin teklifiniz. Ben siyaseti etik değerler üzerinden okuyorum, siz mavra yapabilirsiniz. Bu sizin Sayın Cumhurbaşkanımızın, ilahî liderinizin vaadi, siz vadettiniz bunu Türkiye'ye, bu sizin vaadiniz.
EJDER AÇIKKAPI (Elâzığ) - Doğru konuş bak.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Tamam.
EJDER AÇIKKAPI (Elâzığ) - Doğru konuş.
SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - Ya o kadar şeye ses çıkarmadınız...
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Temiz suya erişemiyorlar, okul yemeğine erişemiyorlar.
(AK PARTİ sıralarından laf atmalar, gürültüler)
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Büyük liderinizin...
EJDER AÇIKKAPI (Elâzığ) - Bağırma! Bağırma!
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Sen böyle susturmaya çalış.
Büyük liderinizin toplam maliyeti 225 milyar lira...
(AK PARTİ sıralarından laf atmalar, gürültüler)
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Arkadaşlar, işi boğuntuya getirmeyin.
225 milyar lira okul temizlik görevlisine...
(AK PARTİ sıralarından laf atmalar, gürültüler)
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Böyle devam edecek miyiz? Böyle mi devam edeceğiz?
Güvenlik, temizlik görevlisi ve okul sağlığı hemşiresinin...
(AK PARTİ ve CHP milletvekilleri arasında karşılıklı laf atmalar, gürültüler)
OTURUM BAŞKANI ORHAN ERDEM - Arkadaşlar, bir dakika.
Suat Bey, bir dakika.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Ağabey, zamanım geçiyor.
OTURUM BAŞKANI ORHAN ERDEM - Siz de dikkat edin, "ilahî lider" ifadesi çok yanlış oldu, bunu düzeltelim.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Doğru.
OTURUM BAŞKANI ORHAN ERDEM - Arkadaşlar, lütfen.
EJDER AÇIKKAPI (Elâzığ) - Ne demek ya!
OTURUM BAŞKANI ORHAN ERDEM - Ejder Bey, lütfen.
Buyurun.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Efendim şöyle, bunu düzeltelim hemen: "İlahî lider" dediğim "büyük lider" olarak görüyorsunuz anlamında söylemiştim, kırdıysam özür dilerim çünkü konuşmamız gereken önemli konular var.
OTURUM BAŞKANI ORHAN ERDEM - Teşekkür ederiz, tamam.
(AK PARTİ sıralarından laf atmalar, gürültüler)
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Ya böyle olmaz Başkanım, sürekli aynı numara.
VELİ AĞBABA (Malatya) - Sen devam et.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Temizlik görevlisi, güvenlik görevlisi, sağlık görevlisinin toplam maliyeti 225 milyar, okul yemeğinin toplam maliyeti 235 milyar; toplamda 450 milyar. Sizin bu sene -çok kızıyorsunuz ya- yandaş şirketlerden almamaya karar verdiğiniz vergi 768 milyar.
İSMAİL GÜNEŞ (Uşak) - Bunu ispatla.
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - 1 tane şirket ismi verir misin?
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Yandaş şirketlerden alacağınız 768 milyarı... Sevgili Bakanımız, Sayın Bakanımız Yusuf Tekin'e 450 milyar lira verin, 450 milyar lira, devam etsin.
CEVDET AKAY (Karabük) - Vergi harcamaları olarak var ya...
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - 15 bin mülakatla atama yaptınız "Yapmayacağız." dediğiniz şeyleri yaptınız. Öğretmenlerin maaşlarının yüzde 70'ine denk geliyordu öğretim yılına hazırlık ödeneği, bunu yüzde 10'una indirdiniz. Ücretli öğretmenlikle ilgili söyledim.
(AK PARTİ sıralarından laf atmalar, gürültüler)
AHMET VEHBİ BAKIRLIOĞLU (Manisa) - Vergi harcamalarından bahsediyor ya.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Vergi harcamalarından bahsediyorum.
Dolayısıyla, sayın arkadaşlarım, Türkiye'de büyük skandallar yaşanıyor. PISA'yla ilgili Bakan sonuçları söylüyor, sadece zamanım olmadığı için söyleyeyim: 2003'e göre her aşamada geridesiniz; 2003 ile 2022'yi karşılaştırırsanız Sayın Bakan, her noktada geridesiniz, matematikte de fende de geridesiniz.
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - Başkanım, benim dakikalarımdan üç dakika beyefendiye verin.
VELİ AĞBABA (Malatya) - Konuşma, ben vereceğim.
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - Şu kesinleşmiş vergisi affedilen şirketlerin ismini, 1 tanesini o üç dakikada söylesin.
VELİ AĞBABA (Malatya) - Ben vereceğim, merak etme sen.
OTURUM BAŞKANI ORHAN ERDEM - Suat Bey, iki dakika ek süre veriyorum.
Buyurun.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Şimdi, değerli arkadaşlar, üniversitelerdeki sorunlara giremiyoruz bile, çok fazla sorunlarımız var. Burada şunu söylemek gerekir: Büyük bir dijital kriz yaşandı; e-devlet krizi yaşandı, e-imza krizi yaşandı. Burada Millî Eğitim Bakanlığının ve Yükseköğretim Kurumunun e-devlet şifreleri, e-imzaları çalındı; Allah'a şükür, hiç kimse istifa etmedi. Yüzlerce insanın, binlerce insanın fazladan diploma aldığı oldu, kimse bununla ilgili dönüp cevap vermedi.
"Proje okullar 3'ü, 5i geçmez." demiştiniz, sayıları 2.153 oldu. Bakan nasıl yapıyor bilmiyorum, 80 bin öğretmeni ve 5 bin yöneticiyi bizzat kendisi atıyor. Nasıl tanıyor bu insanları, nasıl birbirlerini arasından seçiyor, bilmiyorum. Şimdi de bu okulları birtakım vakıfların, derneklerin himayesine verecekmiş.
AYYÜCE TÜRKEŞ TAŞ (Adana) - Yapay zekâ...
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Şunu da söyleyelim: 29 Ekimde Cumhurbaşkanlarının resimlerinin okullara asılması yasa dışıdır. Bu ne Tören ve Kutlamalar Yönetmeliği'ne ne Türk Bayrağı Kanunu'na uygun değildir.
Sayın Bakan, bir kez daha size söylüyorum, bir kez daha istirham ediyorum: 10 Kasım Atatürk'ü Anma Haftası'nı, 10 Kasım Atatürk'ü Anma Günü'nü tatile getirmeyin. Siz Bakan olduğunuzdan bu yana bu böyle. Türkiye'de tarihî olarak Atatürk hepimizin Atatürk'üdür. 10 Kasımı ara tatile denk getirmeyin. 10 Kasımı ara tatile getirmek size hiçbir şey kazandırmaz.
LGS'yle ilgili olarak çok şey tartıştık. Sonuç şudur: Söylediğimizin hepsinin gerçek olduğu ortaya çıktı. Sayısal ile sözelin sınav bitmeden yayınlandığı, sözelin hemen sonrasında, yetmiş iki dakika sonra, sayısalın sınav devam ederken yayınlandığı, bir kısım insanların tutuklandığı, bir genel müdürün görevden alındığı ortaya çıktı ama bununla ilgili hiçbir tasarruf uygulanmadı.
Dolayısıyla okullara ÇEDES Projesi'yle birilerini sokmak ve başka siyasi partilerin yan kuruluşlarını eğitim kurumlarına
sokmak yanlıştır, bu uygulamalardan vazgeçin. Türkiye'nin eğitim alanında ciddi atılımlara ihtiyacı var. Yapılanlar için evet ama yapılacak çok iş var. Eğitim Türkiye'nin beka sorunudur.
Teşekkür ediyorum.
records.englishTranslation:
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Mr. President, esteemed members of parliament, esteemed Minister and esteemed bureaucrats; Welcome all.
We will discuss the 2026 education budget. The education budget is not just any budget; It is the most fundamental mirror that shows a country's understanding of justice, its development vision, the value it attaches to its children, and its bond with the contemporary world. The education budget is also the fate of millions of children. Gazi Mustafa Kemal Atatürk said, "Education is the one that either makes a nation live as a free, glorious, independent and high community, or leaves it in slavery and misery." he expressed. The education budget is what will determine whether children will have access to the right to quality education, whether teachers will live below the poverty line, whether village schools will remain open, whether children in rural areas will be able to go to school, whether schools will be resistant to disasters and earthquakes, and whether parents will go into debt to send their children to school.
Every year, we discuss the budget here together with the ministers and bureaucrats of the period. There is a government that has been going on for a quarter of a century, this government has practices, it has consequences for which it is personally responsible. When we look at the budget of the Ministry of National Education, we see that it remains almost the same as the TÜİK inflation rate compared to last year. As you know, TURKSTAT inflation is 32.9 percent, ENAG inflation is 60 percent. As the Republican People's Party, I would like to call on the government once again to test which data are correct in any independent commission to be established. If TUIK's data is correct, it means that the figures have remained the same, meaning nothing has been added to the budget compared to last year. Of course, we need to look at what this means. We should not use statistics to our advantage; for example,
The fact that the Justice and Development Party government ranked the Ministry of National Education first among the ministries does not show how much share it received, but shows that other ministries lost more budget. For example, the share of education in the gross domestic product of the total budget was 4.18 in 2016, while today it is 3.74; While its share from the central government budget was 19.24, this year it is 15.29. The share of the Ministry of National Education budget in the central government budget was 13.38 in 2016 and 10.26 in 2026. And while the ratio of the Ministry of Education budget to the gross domestic product was 2.91 in 2016, it is 2.51 today, this is the data. 83 percent of this is personnel and social security expenses, 7 percent is the purchase of goods and services, and only 8 percent is capital expenses. Let's look at this: If we are going to make a difference in the budget of the Ministry of National Education, we definitely need investment. The share of this budget allocated to investment in 2002, when the Justice and Development Party came to power, was 17.18 percent. During the period of the late Bülent Ecevit, whom the President did not like, it was 30.63 percent, today it is 8.25 percent. In other words, compared to your own government, it is half, and compared to Ecevit's government, it is 1 in four.
The share of university budgets in gross domestic product was 0.90 in 2016 and 0.84 in 2026. The share of YÖK university budget in the central budget was 4.14 in 2016, and today it is 3.44. The Justice and Development Party is very proud of increasing the number of universities, and so is our President of YÖK.

The number increased from 93 universities, 68 of which were state and 25 foundation, to 208 universities today. So, what good does it do to increase the number of universities if their share is constantly decreasing? If a budget is not given, what is the purpose of increasing the number of universities and the number of students numerically?
As of today, education in Türkiye has very unpleasant data. When we look at Türkiye, we are faced with very serious problems. Some of these are related to the problems created by the Justice and Development Party, some of them are related to the results and solutions it is trying to create for the problems it has created. For example, you are the one who admits that 4+4+4, which is the invention of your government, does not work today and wants to change it. You are the one who talks about the results of a model such as the Türkiye Century Education Model, which you named in our own political party's election manifesto and whose author is unknown, whose source is unknown, and which has not been determined by any measurement and evaluation. You are the one who created the problem of open education and ghost classes. You are the one who created the problem of ÇEDES and religiousization. You are the one who created the problem of children dying in MESEMs. You are the one who has problems in preschool. I'm not even touching on problems such as cleanliness, security, the closure of 20 thousand village schools, infrastructure and equipment deficiencies.
Just a month ago, I made a press statement in the garden of a school. Only 4th grade students can go to the flag ceremony at the school because you put a sign saying "school", but you forgot to put a garden where 4 grades can go to the flag ceremony side by side. Therefore, would you like the signs of 4 different schools in the same school or the signs of 3 different schools? Of course, a twenty-three-year government will build schools, increase the number, and achieve results. I have great respect for this, and we thank those who have ruled a country for twenty-five, twenty-three years, but we cannot ignore the real problems. Or the reputation, personal rights, laddering problems, standard staff problems that our teachers experience, the return of the Teaching Profession Law Proposal from the Constitutional Court and its passage in an almost similar way, and the fact that the President, who is angry about 68 thousand teachers, ignores that the problem of unappointed teachers is 1 million today. Interview victimization, "Don't do it." Even though we said, "We will not conduct an interview." Even though you said and asked for votes, after receiving the votes you had an interview and said "This cannot be done." We said, despite your objection and even though you admitted this by conducting field-based interviews this year, 1,611 teachers continue their lives as interview victims. Who will give back their lives? If they had entered last year with this year's system, perhaps this would not have happened.
There are 100 thousand paid teachers; I ask the esteemed MPs: You are going to the hospital, right? You are taking your children too. Do you call a graduate of the faculty of agriculture, who has not graduated from a medical faculty, a "cardiologist"? Do you enter surgery by calling a mechanical engineer a "surgeon"? Is there such a thing as a paid doctor? How does paid teaching work? How does someone who is not a graduate of the faculty of education, or even someone who is not a four-year school graduate, get in? If you ask the Minister, there is no such thing as paid teaching.

Then, when it was realized that even the digital application was a paid teaching job, the problem was solved when the name was changed. There are nearly 100 thousand people entering the school with additional tuition fees, and you employ them as illegal workers. You work below the minimum wage and you earn an additional 100 thousand
If teachers are needed, you have a shortage of 100 thousand. As if stealing from these people's salaries is not enough, and not paying these people salaries for 12 months of the year is not enough, you fill 100 thousand positions for 1 million teachers waiting to be appointed, with people you employ for less money, less time, and most of them are not qualified; shall we not talk about this?
İSMET GÜNEŞHAN (Çanakkale) - They see the burden on the state.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - You are the one who took away the rights that private sector teachers earned in 1965, the base salary application, in a midnight operation while Mr. Minister was undersecretary. You are the one who makes them work under someone's nose, like a seasonal agricultural worker, with fixed-term contracts of nine or ten months. Does this budget find a solution to these? Is there anyone who can hear these cries? I don't think it is. Or the problems in the inspection and audit system, the problems in the education budget and investment budget that I just mentioned, and unqualified staffing. Being the Provincial Director of National Education, he goes to the Provincial Presidency of the Justice and Development Party and then writes without shame: "I thank our esteemed Member of Parliament, I thank our esteemed Provincial Chairman." saying. If you find this normal, I do not find this normal, I have to say this. I do not find it normal that you give incentives to private schools and the lack of a school budget.
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - Yours are also their relatives, brothers-in-law and managers.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - You can continue like this, Mr. Orhan, if it suits you, you can continue.
Should we not talk about the problems we experience in higher education? Shall we not say the lack of coordination, shall we not say the unplanned expansion? Should we not mention the problem created by rector appointments? The person you call "Rector" is either a former member of parliament from the Justice and Development Party, or his relative, brother-in-law, brother-in-law, brother, or cousin; Is it possible to appoint a rector like this? You came and discussed how the staff of YÖK will be determined, how the 21 people will be determined, even the job canceled by the Constitutional Court, in the Planning and Budget Commission, but you missed it from the Ministry of National Education Commission.
Administrative and financial autonomy brings rights; The personal rights of academics are in decline, and the salaries they receive are the same as in previous years. The problems in the field of housing and scholarships are obvious and I congratulate you, the rate of unemployed university graduates is higher than the rate of unemployed people who are not university graduates only in Türkiye; Isn't it necessary to think and ponder over this? And then we sit and talk about the brain drain, we feel sad, nice Mr. President: "Let them go if they go." But I don't think we can find such doctors easily.
Sometimes the Minister comes out and says: "We have brought the schooling of girls to 98 percent.

" Let me tell you with the data shared by the Minister: Primary school enrollment rate has decreased from 98.9 to 95.4 since 2012, secondary school enrollment rate has decreased from 93.1 to 89.1 - these are not my data, these are your data - at the age of 5 in pre-school - which you promise in every election period so that you will make it compulsory; I do not give it, you do - it has decreased from 84.3 to 82.5, The age of 3-5 has decreased from 51 to 49; the OECD average is 84.9. But if there is compulsory education, they have to be closed, because then the state will enter into the process, so you do not call the age of 5 as compulsory education. As the Republican People's Party, we will make compulsory education for the ages of 4 and 5. You will hear the rest in the program launch tomorrow. It dropped to 82.9 and thus 611,612 of our children have no connection with any school. I ask the Minister and his staff: If you don't worry about this, why don't you ask where are the 77,400 children aged 6-9? You couldn't find one, you couldn't find five, you couldn't find a hundred; 93 thousand children aged 10-13, 440. thousand 14-17 years old; the number of private schools has increased from 4,664 to 14,700. Families are sending their children to private schools so that they can live safely. You boast of this as a success. The state does not invest or spend, you say, "Let those who can pay pay." Public expenditures in OECD and European countries are much higher than in Türkiye, and household expenditures are much lower. You have to decide what you want to keep constant, and then you argue as if you are not the inventor of "4+4+4". You say: "Sir, if we reduce this, the public opinion is like this." Let me tell you: EĞİTİM-BİR-SEN, MÜSİAD, Enstitüsü Sosyal, Enderun Özgün Educators, Conscious Teachers. Maarif, Yeni Akit, Yeni Şafak, Türkiye, Medeniyet Enstitüsü, these are you, I respect you, but what does the Minister say? I'm sorry, I feel very sorry. The Minister says, "Political opponents act as if I have to take their opinions, I don't have to take your opinion." If I were the minister, I wouldn't make such an unfortunate statement, you would take everyone's opinion and of course you would proceed according to your own political opinion.
It is not said like that, even if you are not going to do it, it is not said. Why would this be done? He explains that these Conscious Teachers need intermediate staff. How many intermediate staff are needed? I asked those who work in business life, they said: "1 million." Let me give you a number: You don't care about 611,612 people, they are out of school; 4 million 676 thousand people between the ages of 15-29 are neither in education nor employment, that's 5.5 million for you.

There are 1.6 million students in vocational and technical education - these are the places where you even closed teacher training schools - and 1 million students in open education - open education means no education, by the way - but that's 8 million for you. You couldn't find 1 million intermediate staff out of 8 million, now we shorten the high schools and say "Let's put 1.6 million-2 million more people on the streets." you say. He got addicted to substances, he got involved with peer bullying, he got involved with gangs; You have no problem with these. Thank God, he returned from the Presidential Cabinet and I was very happy. Dear friends, you should not bother with compulsory education of children. Do you know what the result is? Children dragged into crime. 612,651 children; 280 thousand of them were victims, 202 thousand of them were dragged into crime. Yesterday - I have an 11-year-old son - Muhammed Kendirci lost his life in Urfa. This year, 10 children lost their lives in November alone, 82 children died in 2025, 71 children in 2024; 819 children lost their lives in twelve years. 16 children lost their lives in MESEM - while the Minister was Minister - here is Alperen Uygun, he died a week ago, he fell down the elevator shaft and died; He died a week ago, a week ago, and if I were a minister, I would not sit in that seat in a place where 16 children died, friends. I ask the General Manager who is responsible for that vocational training: When will you resign, whatever happens? For example, will you resign when 400 thousand people in MESEMs die? What if you resign?
NURTEN YONTAR (Tekirdağ) - Even so, they will not resign.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Alperen Uygun died, there was another Alperen in Soma; Alperen died, his sentence was given, do you know what happened? The owner - he was also working elsewhere, you did not supervise him, he appeared to be the owner of the workplace - gave 36 thousand liras and left, his master gave 15 thousand liras and left; They did not serve time in prison. God also punished us! Alperen Enes Ural died, the man who owned the shop paid 36 thousand liras, his master paid 15 thousand liras; His mother Selda and father Faruk had nothing left but to cry. I saw his grave - because you didn't see it, you didn't go - I went and said, "Let him rest next to his grandfather." They said, it is such a small place because two graves are side by side; They buried it there, they buried it there. When I look for their mothers and fathers when those children die, my heart hurts, I cry, but I can't call them; This is my MESSAGE, my MESEM is a massacre. There is no such system as four days at work and one day at school. You learn business at school, you will go there, you will look at how the vocational and technical school built by Ekrem İmamoğlu, whom you are very angry with, is structured in Elazığ, you will see. MESEM is an infanticide mechanism. Children are dying in schools, teachers are dying, principals are dying, being killed. They enter the school, take the child's pocket money, and the principal says, "Don't do it." he says, and they break the deputy principal's nose. Well, why? There is not 1 security guard in the schools. It's in the land registry, it's in your offices, it's in the ministries' offices, it's in the civil registry office; not in schools. I asked the Ministry, and the answer came on September 10: "There are private law enforcement officers in 1,032 schools." 1,032 schools, a significant portion of which are related to the PIKTES project funded from abroad, that is, Türkiye has not put security guards in the places where the citizens who need to be protected are the number one.

I submitted a proposal, I submitted a budget proposal now, you can approve it; I submitted a bill, you can approve it. If we cannot put a security guard in schools, we should not go around saying that we are members of parliament.
We discussed and discussed with the Minister about the cleaning; You thought you would employ personnel for 8 thousand liras, you thought you would hire 120 thousand people; You didn't apologize either. Then "Let us clean the schools and help." We said, "No, don't do that either." you said. After all, Mr. President said today - this is the Minister's statement - that you will hire 70 thousand people, but you will not hire them as permanent staff? And what is your hostility towards workers? Take 70 thousand people on staff. There were janitors in our childhood, they were the history of the school. Why don't you buy it, janitor? Why don't you hire the son of your country for whom you pay 40 thousand, 50 thousand, 60 thousand, 70 thousand lira salary?
There is a decision of the Council, it says in the decision of the Council: "Hire librarians, health personnel (nurses), technical personnel, cleaning and security personnel." We need to hire 65 thousand security personnel and 100 thousand cleaning personnel. We need to recruit 75 thousand school health nurses, they will intervene in accidents at school. Isn't it present in the ministries where we all are? An ambulance is standing at the door, a paramedic is standing at the door; You pass laws, it stops in tiny companies. Why isn't there 1 health nurse in a place with 1,000 students? We need to discuss this, the budget needs to correspond to this. We give our proposal; Go ahead, reject it again. Who will monitor students with chronic illnesses? Who will respond to school accidents? Who will monitor medications and similar treatments? Who will look after health education, hygiene education, healthy living, nutrition education? School health nurses will look after it. children to schools
They are going hungry, hungry. "They don't go hungry." If anyone says, let's go together, there are organizations in this country.
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - Let's see, let's go.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - It is offered to the Minister, 235 billion liras. By the way, you have - I'm very sorry - 2 Justice and Development Parties; One always promises, the other doesn't, it's your thing. You say, you wrote - there is also a picture of Mr. President - and he says: "We are giving it to so many people, we will make it widespread." Mr. Minister became Minister, the first thing he did was cancel Mahmut Özer's pre-school meal. Now they asked, "Why don't you give it?" "I swear, we looked at this from an academic perspective, but it doesn't work. We don't have the staff to cook the food. If there were staff, we wouldn't have a place to provide the food. Even if we did - I asked private schools - the children wouldn't eat it. Children wouldn't eat it anyway." he said. I say, open it up, guys.
YAŞAR KIRKPINAR (İzmir) - Very beautiful.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Isn't it a beautiful picture? This is your offer. I read politics through ethical values, you can do it. This is the promise of your esteemed President, your divine leader, you promised this to Türkiye, this is your promise.
EJDER AÇIKKAPI (Elâzığ) - Speak correctly.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - OK.
EJDER AÇIKKAPI (Elâzığ) - Speak correctly.
SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - You didn't say anything about all that.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - They cannot access clean water, they cannot access school meals.

(Quotes and noises from AK PARTY ranks)
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Your great leader.
EJDER AÇIKKAPI (Elâzığ) - Don't shout! Yell!
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Try to silence him like this.
The total cost of your great leader is 225 billion liras.
(Quotes and noises from AK PARTY ranks)
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Friends, do not make things difficult.
225 billion lira for school cleaners.
(Quotes and noises from AK PARTY ranks)
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Will we continue like this? Will we continue like this?
Security, cleaning staff and school health nurse.
(Mutual exchanges and noises between AK PARTY and CHP MPs)
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Friends, wait a minute.
Mr. Suat, wait a minute.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Brother, my time is running out.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Pay attention, the expression "divine leader" is very wrong, let's correct this.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - That's right.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Friends, please.
EJDER AÇIKKAPI (Elâzığ) - What does it mean!
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Mr. Ejder, please.
Here you go.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Sir, let's correct this immediately: I meant "divine leader" in the sense that you see him as a "great leader". I apologize if I offended you, because we have important issues to talk about.
CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Thank you, okay.
(Quotes and noises from AK PARTY ranks)
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - It can't be like this, President, it's always the same number.
VELİ AĞBABA (Malatya) - You go on.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - The total cost of cleaning staff, security guards and healthcare workers is 225 billion, the total cost of school meals is 235 billion; 450 billion in total. The tax you decided not to collect from partisan companies this year - you are so angry - is 768 billion.
İSMAİL GÜNEŞ (Uşak) - Prove it.
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - Can you name 1 company?
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - 768 billion you will receive from pro-government companies. Dear Minister, give 450 billion liras to our esteemed Minister Yusuf Tekin, 450 billion liras, let it continue.
CEVDET AKAY (Karabük) - As for tax expenditures.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - You made the appointment after 15 thousand interviews. "We will not do it." You did what you said. The preparation allowance for the academic year was equivalent to 70 percent of teachers' salaries, but you reduced it to 10 percent. I talked about paid teaching.
(Quotes and noises from AK PARTY ranks)
AHMET VEHBİ BAKIRLIOĞLU (Manisa) - He is talking about tax expenditures.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - I am talking about tax expenditures.
Therefore, my dear friends, there are big scandals in Türkiye. The Minister says the results regarding PISA, let me just say it because I don't have time: You are behind at every stage compared to 2003; If you compare 2003 and 2022, Mr. Minister, you are behind at every point, both in mathematics and science.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - President, give the gentleman three minutes of my time.
VELİ AĞBABA (Malatya) - Don't talk, I will give it to you.
ORHAN YEGİN (Ankara) - Let him tell you the name of one of the companies whose final taxes have been forgiven in those three minutes.
VELİ AĞBABA (Malatya) - I will give it to you, don't worry.

CHAIRMAN OF THE SESSION ORHAN ERDEM - Mr. Suat, I give two minutes of additional time.
Here you go.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Now, dear friends, we cannot even delve into the problems in universities, we have too many problems. It should be said here: There was a major digital crisis; There was an e-government crisis, there was an e-signature crisis. Here, the e-government passwords and e-signatures of the Ministry of National Education and the Higher Education Institution were stolen; Thank God, no one resigned. Hundreds, thousands of people received extra diplomas, but no one responded to this.
"Project schools do not exceed 3 or 5 students." You said, their number was 2,153. I don't know how the Minister does it, he personally appoints 80 thousand teachers and 5 thousand administrators. I don't know how he knows these people, how he chooses each other. Now he will give these schools under the protection of some foundations and associations.
AYYÜCE TÜRKEŞ TAŞ (Adana) - Artificial intelligence.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Let's also say this: It is illegal to hang the pictures of the Presidents in schools on October 29. This is neither in compliance with the Ceremonies and Celebrations Regulation nor the Turkish Flag Law.
Dear Minister, I am telling you once again, I request you once again: Do not make 10 November Atatürk Commemoration Week or 10 November Atatürk Commemoration Day a holiday. It has been like this since you became Minister. Historically in Türkiye, Atatürk is the Atatürk of all of us. Don't make November 10th a midterm break. Bringing November 10th to a midterm break will not gain you anything.
We discussed a lot about LGS. The conclusion is this: Everything we said turned out to be true. It was revealed that the numerical and verbal were published before the end of the exam, that the numerical was published immediately after the oral, seventy-two minutes later, while the exam was still ongoing, that some people were arrested, and that a general manager was dismissed, but no action was taken regarding this.
Therefore, bringing people into schools with the ÇEDES Project and subsidiaries of other political parties into educational institutions
It is wrong to sting, give up these practices. Türkiye needs serious breakthroughs in the field of education. Yes, for what has been done, but there is a lot of work to be done. Education is Türkiye's survival problem.
Thank you.

records.title: Komisyonun çalışma takvimine, Komisyonda görevlendirilecek uzmanların belirlenmesine ve Komisyona davet edileceklerin tespitine ilişkin görüşme münasebetiyle

fields.Subject: Komisyonun çalışma takvimine, Komisyonda görevlendirilecek uzmanların belirlenmesine ve Komisyona davet edileceklerin tespitine ilişkin görüşme münasebetiyle

records.originalTurkish:
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Değerli Başkanım, takvimle ilgili olarak Komisyonun takdiri önemli eğer "Her hafta toplanılsın." denirse, öyle yapacağız elbette fakat bu günle ilgili olarak da çarşamba gününü seçmişsiniz, buna da adapte oluruz ama mesela, bu hafta benim İstanbul'da büyük bir çalıştayım var yani Türkiye'deki bütün belediyeleri İstanbul'da topluyoruz biz.
BAŞKAN CENGİZ AYDOĞDU - Bu hafta zaten bu toplantımız bitti.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Bu şey tartışmasından bağımsız yani bir buçuk ay önce belirlediğimiz bir şey o da üstüne geldi şu anda. Mesela, ben burada olamam yani haftaya çarşamba olamam çünkü ben salı toplanıyoruz diye düşünerek zaten günü öyle belirlemiştik. Dolayısıyla en azından orada bir es verilebilirse... Bu, bir kişisel pozisyon olabilir, onu da söyleyeyim ama hani ben Komisyonun bu ilk toplantısından itibaren
olmak isterim, onu söyleyeyim. Biz vekillerimizle birlikte gelecek hafta salı akşam saatlerine kadar, bugün tartışmaktan ziyade bir çerçeveyi yani şu, şu, şu konu başlıklarında görüşülmesi ve şu uzmanları -ki o çalışmayı yaptık- şu STK'leri ve kamu kurumlarının dinlenmesi diye 4 ayrı başlıkta size bir rapor sunarız efendim.
BAŞKAN CENGİZ AYDOĞDU - Tamam, teşekkür ediyorum.
records.englishTranslation:
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Dear President, the Commission's discretion regarding the calendar is important if it says "It should meet every week." If asked, of course we will do that, but you have chosen Wednesday for this day, we will adapt to this, but for example, I have a big workshop in Istanbul this week, so we are gathering all the municipalities in Türkiye in Istanbul.
PRESIDENT CENGİZ AYDOĞDU - Our meeting has already ended this week.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - This thing is independent of the discussion, it is something we determined a month and a half ago and now it has come to an end. For example, we had already determined the day by thinking that I cannot be here, that is, next week on Wednesday, because we are meeting on Tuesday. Therefore, at least if a break can be given there... This may be a personal position, let me tell you that, but as of this first meeting of the Commission,
I want to be, let me tell you that. We, together with our deputies, will present you a report under four different headings, namely, to discuss a framework on this, that, and such topics, and to listen to these experts - we have done that work - these NGOs and public institutions, rather than discussing it today, by Tuesday evening next week.
MAYOR CENGİZ AYDOĞDU - Okay, thank you.
records.englishTitle: On the occasion of the meeting regarding the working calendar of the Commission, the determination of the experts to be assigned to the Commission and the determination of those to be invited to the Commission.

englishFields.Subject: On the occasion of the meeting regarding the working calendar of the Commission, the determination of the experts to be assigned to the Commission and the determination of those to be invited to the Commission.

records.title: Profesör Doktor Betül Ulukol ve Doçent Doktor Nilüfer Koçtürk’ün yaptıkları sunumlara ilişkin görüşme

fields.Subject: Profesör Doktor Betül Ulukol ve Doçent Doktor Nilüfer Koçtürk’ün yaptıkları sunumlara ilişkin görüşme

records.originalTurkish:
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Çok teşekkür ederim Sayın Başkan.
Değerli hocalarıma çok teşekkür ederim; Betül Hocama, Nilüfer Hocama çok teşekkür ediyorum, çok yararlandım.
0-18 yaş grubunda Türkiye'de, istatistiklere göre, 22,5 milyon civarında küçük yurttaşımız var ve bunlar ülke nüfusunun yüzde 26,5'ini oluşturuyorlar; Avrupa'daki 27 ülkeden fazla, dolayısıyla başlı başına bir ülke. Ve maalesef, bizim bu çocuklarımızı korumak konusunda çok sıkıntımız var ki bütün partiler ortak olarak bu Komisyonu kurduk. Siz de söylediniz, bu Komisyon da defaatle kuruluyor, rapor yazıyor, sonra tekrar ihtiyaç duyuluyor. Narin'i koruyamadık. Narin'den sonra, Sıla bebekten sonra -ki hassasiyet de oluşmuştu- bebekleri koruyamadığımızı anladık, küçücük bebeklerin kâr amacıyla öldürüldüğünü öğrendik.
Çocuk anneler sorunumuz var, konuşmaya bile korkuyoruz, istatistiklerini bile tam olarak öğrenemediğimiz çocuk anneler sorunumuz var Türkiye'de. Dün ben isyan ettim, burada bir basın toplantısı yaptım.
TUBA KÖKSAL (Kahramanmaraş) - "Çocuk"la "anne"yi yan yana kullanmayalım.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Kullanmayalım, peki, nasıl yapalım? Çocukken anne olmuşlar.
TUBA KÖKSAL (Kahramanmaraş) - Tamamen istismara uğramışlar.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - İşte, ne yapalım, evet yani sonuçta bakmak zorunda oldukları çocukları var. Uygun bir terminolojiyi hocalarımız söylerse sevinirim.
Dün bir MESEM öğrencimizin davası vardı. 17 yaşında bir çocuğumuz cumartesi günü çalışmaması gereken bir yerde 2'nci kattan düştü, öldü. O çocuğumuzun işinden sorumlu olan patron, başka bir yerde sigortalı iken iş yeri açıp 36.400 lirayla kurtardı, usta öğreticisi de 15.200 lirayla kurtardı. Yani bir çocuk öldü -yani benim çocuğumun başına gelse Allah göstermesin ne yapacağımı bilemiyorum- 17 yaşında bir ana kuzusu öldü, biri 15 bin lirayla, öbürü 36 bin lirayla kurtardı.
Toplumun genelinde de inanılmaz bir şiddet meselesi var, ben de vekilime katılıyorum, medya da bunda çok etkili yani sürekli "Kurtlar Vadisi" şeklinde dolaşan bir medya ortamı da var. Dolayısıyla, ülkemizde çocuklar büyük bir tehdit altında.
Ben sizden şunu duydum: "Bir strateji ve eylem planı konusunda, yasal çerçeve konusunda, sosyoekonomik ve sosyokültürel faktörler meselesi üzerinden, kurumsal ve mesleki eksiklikler üzerinden, eğitime dair konular, veri eksikliği ve raporlama üzerinden çok sayıda eksiklik var, yapılması gereken iş var; yapılan olumlu işler de var ama çok daha fazla yol var gidilmesi gereken." diye duydum.
Şimdi, burada, tabii, 3 Kasım 2016'da bir rapor hazırlamış, muhtemelen o rapordan bahsediyorsunuz. Ben rapora baktım, arkadaşlarımla da çalıştık, bayağı iyi bir rapor yani gerçekten. Ben bizden önceki milletvekillerine teşekkür ederim. Onun da adı benzer, bir farkı var sadece: Başta Cinsel İstismar Olmak Üzere Çocuklara Yönelik Her Türlü İstismar Olaylarının Araştırılarak Alınması Gereken Önlemlerin Belirlenmesi Amacıyla Kurulan Meclis Araştırma Komisyonu. İktidarıyla muhalefetiyle oturmuşuz, çalışmışız, dört başı mamur bir rapor hazırlanmış ama sonuç itibarıyla o günden bugüne iyiye gittiğimizi düşünmüyorum, bu benim düşüncem. Burada uzun siyasi tahliller yapmak da mümkün, bunun bir yararı olduğunu da düşünmüyorum. Örneğin, yani bana göre ülkede
kaynak var ama o kaynakları nereye tahsis ettiğinizle ilgili. Bana göre, uzman var -ben masanın öbür tarafında da çok oturmuş birisiyim, sivil toplum kökenli birisiyim, psikoloğum vesaire- birikim de var. Bizim meselemizin bunu nasıl hayata geçireceğimiz konusunda olması gerekiyor.
Bir: "Strateji ve eylem planı yok." dediniz Hocam, bununla ilgili ne tavsiye edersiniz, nasıl başlamak lazım?
İki: Bir fark yaratmamız lazım yani ben milletvekillerinin hangi partiden olursa olsun burada iyi niyetle hizmet ettiklerini görüyorum. Bazen hiç anlaşamıyoruz, bağırıp çağırıyoruz ama iyi niyetle bir şey yapmaya çalışan insanlar fakat yetmiyor. Farkı nasıl yaratırız? Mesela, çocuklara yönelik yepyeni bir yasa mı? Mesela, ÇİM'ler için olumlu şeyler söylüyoruz. Bir, kapsayıcı değil yeterince; iki, sadece Sağlık Bakanlığıyla ilgili bir konu. Hâlbuki Türkiye'de çocuk hizmetlerinin önemli bir kısmı, hatta önlemeyle ilgili işler Aile ve Sosyal Politikalar Bakanlığıyla ilgili. Üstelik ÇİM'ler daha çok cinsel istismarla ilgili bir konu, oysaki istismarın -siz de anlattınız, biz de daha çok öğrendik, bilgilerimizi tazeledik- çok farklı boyutları var. Dolayısıyla daha kapsayıcı bir kurumsal öneri var mıdır, düşünce var mıdır, yapılabilecek bir şey var mıdır ya da tartışılması gereken bir yol var mıdır, onu sormak isterim. Çünkü ben meseleyi bir stratejik eylem planının olmamasına, yasal çerçevenin, kurumsal yapının ve personel başta olmak üzere kapasite eksikliğine ve arkadan da önleme, koruma, güçlendirme alanındaki politikaların eksikliğine yoruyorum. Buralarda uzmanlar olarak -ben bazen kendimi öyle buluyorum, yıllarca o tarafta konuşup sonra buraya gelince- nereden başlasak, nasıl yapsak? Çünkü buradaki iktidar partisi milletvekillerinin de muhalefet milletvekillerinin de bu Komisyondan sonuç almak niyetimiz var. Bize uzmanlar olarak ne söylersiniz diye sormak isterim.
Saygılar sunarım
records.englishTranslation:
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Thank you very much, Mr. President.
I would like to thank my dear teachers; I would like to thank my teacher Betül and teacher Nilüfer very much, I benefited a lot.
According to statistics, we have around 22.5 million minor citizens in Türkiye in the 0-18 age group and they constitute 26.5 percent of the country's population; It is more than 27 countries in Europe, so it is a country in itself. And unfortunately, we have so much trouble protecting these children that we established this Commission together with all parties. As you said, this Commission is established many times, reports are written, and then it is needed again. We couldn't protect Narin. After Narin, after baby Sıla - and there was sensitivity - we realized that we could not protect babies, we learned that tiny babies were being killed for profit.
We have a problem of child mothers in Türkiye, we are afraid to even talk about it, we have a problem of child mothers whose statistics we do not even know exactly. Yesterday I rebelled and held a press conference here.
TUBA KÖKSAL (Kahramanmaraş) - Let's not use "child" and "mother" together.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Let's not use it, but how can we do it? They became mothers when they were children.
TUBA KÖKSAL (Kahramanmaraş) - They were completely abused.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Well, what should we do, yes, after all, they have children to take care of. I would be happy if our teachers could tell us the appropriate terminology.
Yesterday, one of our MESEM students had a case. A 17-year-old boy fell from the 2nd floor and died in a place where he was not supposed to be working on Saturday. The boss responsible for our child's job opened a business while he was insured elsewhere and saved him with 36,400 liras, and the master trainer saved him with 15,200 liras. So a child died - I mean, if it happened to my child, God forbid, I don't know what to do - a 17-year-old mother's son died, one of them saved him with 15 thousand liras, the other with 36 thousand liras.
There is an incredible issue of violence in society in general, I agree with my deputy, the media is also very effective in this, that is, there is a media environment that constantly circulates in the form of "Valley of the Wolves". Therefore, children in our country are under great threat.
I heard this from you: "There are many deficiencies regarding a strategy and action plan, the legal framework, the issue of socioeconomic and sociocultural factors, institutional and professional deficiencies, educational issues, lack of data and reporting, there is work to be done; there is also positive work being done, but there is a lot more to go." I heard.
Now, here, of course, he prepared a report on November 3, 2016, you are probably talking about that report. I looked at the report, we also worked with my friends, it is a pretty good report, really. I would like to thank the MPs before us. Its name is similar, there is only one difference: The Parliamentary Investigation Commission Established to Investigate All Kinds of Abuse Cases Against Children, Especially Sexual Abuse, and Determine the Precautions to be Taken. We sat down and worked with the government and the opposition, a complete report was prepared, but as a result, I do not think that we have improved since then, this is my opinion.

It is also possible to make long political analyzes here, but I do not think it is of any use. For example, in my opinion, in the country
There are resources, but it depends on where you allocate those resources. In my opinion, there is an expert - I am a well-established person on the other side of the table, I have a civil society background, I am a psychologist, etc. - there is also experience. Our issue should be about how we will implement this.
One: "There is no strategy or action plan." You said, Sir, what do you recommend about this, how should we start?
Two: We need to make a difference. I see that MPs serve here in good faith, regardless of their party. Sometimes we don't get along, we yell and scream, but people try to do something with good intentions, but it's not enough. How do we make the difference? For example, a brand new law for children? For example, we say positive things about GRASSES. One, it's not comprehensive enough; Secondly, it is an issue related only to the Ministry of Health. However, in Türkiye, a significant part of child services, even prevention-related work, is related to the Ministry of Family and Social Policies. Moreover, CIMs are an issue mostly related to sexual abuse, whereas abuse - as you explained, we learned more and refreshed our knowledge - has many different dimensions. Therefore, I would like to ask if there is a more comprehensive institutional proposal, is there an idea, is there anything that can be done or is there a way that should be discussed? Because I attribute the issue to the lack of a strategic action plan, the lack of capacity, especially the legal framework, institutional structure and personnel, and the lack of policies in the field of prevention, protection and strengthening. As experts around here - I sometimes find myself like that, after talking on that side for years and then coming here - where should we start, how should we do it? Because we intend to get results from this Commission, both the ruling party deputies and the opposition deputies here. I would like to ask what you would tell us as experts.
Best regards

records.title: Profesör Doktor Betül Ulukol ve Doçent Doktor Nilüfer Koçtürk’ün yaptıkları sunumlara ilişkin görüşme münasebetiyle

fields.Subject: Profesör Doktor Betül Ulukol ve Doçent Doktor Nilüfer Koçtürk’ün yaptıkları sunumlara ilişkin görüşme münasebetiyle

records.originalTurkish:
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Çok teşekkür ederim Sayın Başkan.
Değerli hocalarıma çok teşekkür ederim; Betül Hocama, Nilüfer Hocama çok teşekkür ediyorum, çok yararlandım.
0-18 yaş grubunda Türkiye'de, istatistiklere göre, 22,5 milyon civarında küçük yurttaşımız var ve bunlar ülke nüfusunun yüzde 26,5'ini oluşturuyorlar; Avrupa'daki 27 ülkeden fazla, dolayısıyla başlı başına bir ülke. Ve maalesef, bizim bu çocuklarımızı korumak konusunda çok sıkıntımız var ki bütün partiler ortak olarak bu Komisyonu kurduk. Siz de söylediniz, bu Komisyon da defaatle kuruluyor, rapor yazıyor, sonra tekrar ihtiyaç duyuluyor. Narin'i koruyamadık. Narin'den sonra, Sıla bebekten sonra -ki hassasiyet de oluşmuştu- bebekleri koruyamadığımızı anladık, küçücük bebeklerin kâr amacıyla öldürüldüğünü öğrendik.
Çocuk anneler sorunumuz var, konuşmaya bile korkuyoruz, istatistiklerini bile tam olarak öğrenemediğimiz çocuk anneler sorunumuz var Türkiye'de. Dün ben isyan ettim, burada bir basın toplantısı yaptım.
TUBA KÖKSAL (Kahramanmaraş) - "Çocuk"la "anne"yi yan yana kullanmayalım.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Kullanmayalım, peki, nasıl yapalım? Çocukken anne olmuşlar.
TUBA KÖKSAL (Kahramanmaraş) - Tamamen istismara uğramışlar.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - İşte, ne yapalım, evet yani sonuçta bakmak zorunda oldukları çocukları var. Uygun bir terminolojiyi hocalarımız söylerse sevinirim.
Dün bir MESEM öğrencimizin davası vardı. 17 yaşında bir çocuğumuz cumartesi günü çalışmaması gereken bir yerde 2'nci kattan düştü, öldü. O çocuğumuzun işinden sorumlu olan patron, başka bir yerde sigortalı iken iş yeri açıp 36.400 lirayla kurtardı, usta öğreticisi de 15.200 lirayla kurtardı. Yani bir çocuk öldü -yani benim çocuğumun başına gelse Allah göstermesin ne yapacağımı bilemiyorum- 17 yaşında bir ana kuzusu öldü, biri 15 bin lirayla, öbürü 36 bin lirayla kurtardı.
Toplumun genelinde de inanılmaz bir şiddet meselesi var, ben de vekilime katılıyorum, medya da bunda çok etkili yani sürekli "Kurtlar Vadisi" şeklinde dolaşan bir medya ortamı da var. Dolayısıyla, ülkemizde çocuklar büyük bir tehdit altında.
Ben sizden şunu duydum: "Bir strateji ve eylem planı konusunda, yasal çerçeve konusunda, sosyoekonomik ve sosyokültürel faktörler meselesi üzerinden, kurumsal ve mesleki eksiklikler üzerinden, eğitime dair konular, veri eksikliği ve raporlama üzerinden çok sayıda eksiklik var, yapılması gereken iş var; yapılan olumlu işler de var ama çok daha fazla yol var gidilmesi gereken." diye duydum.
Şimdi, burada, tabii, 3 Kasım 2016'da bir rapor hazırlamış, muhtemelen o rapordan bahsediyorsunuz. Ben rapora baktım, arkadaşlarımla da çalıştık, bayağı iyi bir rapor yani gerçekten. Ben bizden önceki milletvekillerine teşekkür ederim. Onun da adı benzer, bir farkı var sadece: Başta Cinsel İstismar Olmak Üzere Çocuklara Yönelik Her Türlü İstismar Olaylarının Araştırılarak Alınması Gereken Önlemlerin Belirlenmesi Amacıyla Kurulan Meclis Araştırma Komisyonu. İktidarıyla muhalefetiyle oturmuşuz, çalışmışız, dört başı mamur bir rapor hazırlanmış ama sonuç itibarıyla o günden bugüne iyiye gittiğimizi düşünmüyorum, bu benim düşüncem. Burada uzun siyasi tahliller yapmak da mümkün, bunun bir yararı olduğunu da düşünmüyorum. Örneğin, yani bana göre ülkede
kaynak var ama o kaynakları nereye tahsis ettiğinizle ilgili. Bana göre, uzman var -ben masanın öbür tarafında da çok oturmuş birisiyim, sivil toplum kökenli birisiyim, psikoloğum vesaire- birikim de var. Bizim meselemizin bunu nasıl hayata geçireceğimiz konusunda olması gerekiyor.
Bir: "Strateji ve eylem planı yok." dediniz Hocam, bununla ilgili ne tavsiye edersiniz, nasıl başlamak lazım?
İki: Bir fark yaratmamız lazım yani ben milletvekillerinin hangi partiden olursa olsun burada iyi niyetle hizmet ettiklerini görüyorum. Bazen hiç anlaşamıyoruz, bağırıp çağırıyoruz ama iyi niyetle bir şey yapmaya çalışan insanlar fakat yetmiyor. Farkı nasıl yaratırız? Mesela, çocuklara yönelik yepyeni bir yasa mı? Mesela, ÇİM'ler için olumlu şeyler söylüyoruz. Bir, kapsayıcı değil yeterince; iki, sadece Sağlık Bakanlığıyla ilgili bir konu. Hâlbuki Türkiye'de çocuk hizmetlerinin önemli bir kısmı, hatta önlemeyle ilgili işler Aile ve Sosyal Politikalar Bakanlığıyla ilgili. Üstelik ÇİM'ler daha çok cinsel istismarla ilgili bir konu, oysaki istismarın -siz de anlattınız, biz de daha çok öğrendik, bilgilerimizi tazeledik- çok farklı boyutları var. Dolayısıyla daha kapsayıcı bir kurumsal öneri var mıdır, düşünce var mıdır, yapılabilecek bir şey var mıdır ya da tartışılması gereken bir yol var mıdır, onu sormak isterim. Çünkü ben meseleyi bir stratejik eylem planının olmamasına, yasal çerçevenin, kurumsal yapının ve personel başta olmak üzere kapasite eksikliğine ve arkadan da önleme, koruma, güçlendirme alanındaki politikaların eksikliğine yoruyorum. Buralarda uzmanlar olarak -ben bazen kendimi öyle buluyorum, yıllarca o tarafta konuşup sonra buraya gelince- nereden başlasak, nasıl yapsak? Çünkü buradaki iktidar partisi milletvekillerinin de muhalefet milletvekillerinin de bu Komisyondan sonuç almak niyetimiz var. Bize uzmanlar olarak ne söylersiniz diye sormak isterim.
Saygılar sunarım
records.englishTranslation:
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Thank you very much, Mr. President.
I would like to thank my dear teachers; I would like to thank my teacher Betül and teacher Nilüfer very much, I benefited a lot.
According to statistics, we have around 22.5 million minor citizens in Türkiye in the 0-18 age group and they constitute 26.5 percent of the country's population; It is more than 27 countries in Europe, so it is a country in itself. And unfortunately, we have so much trouble protecting these children that we established this Commission together with all parties. As you said, this Commission is established many times, reports are written, and then it is needed again. We couldn't protect Narin. After Narin, after baby Sıla - and there was sensitivity - we realized that we could not protect babies, we learned that tiny babies were being killed for profit.
We have a problem of child mothers in Türkiye, we are afraid to even talk about it, we have a problem of child mothers whose statistics we do not even know exactly. Yesterday I rebelled and held a press conference here.
TUBA KÖKSAL (Kahramanmaraş) - Let's not use "child" and "mother" together.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Let's not use it, but how can we do it? They became mothers when they were children.
TUBA KÖKSAL (Kahramanmaraş) - They were completely abused.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Well, what should we do, yes, after all, they have children to take care of. I would be happy if our teachers could tell us the appropriate terminology.
Yesterday, one of our MESEM students had a case. A 17-year-old boy fell from the 2nd floor and died in a place where he was not supposed to be working on Saturday. The boss responsible for our child's job opened a business while he was insured elsewhere and saved him with 36,400 liras, and the master trainer saved him with 15,200 liras. So a child died - I mean, if it happened to my child, God forbid, I don't know what to do - a 17-year-old mother's son died, one of them saved him with 15 thousand liras, the other with 36 thousand liras.
There is an incredible issue of violence in society in general, I agree with my deputy, the media is also very effective in this, that is, there is a media environment that constantly circulates in the form of "Valley of the Wolves". Therefore, children in our country are under great threat.
I heard this from you: "There are many deficiencies regarding a strategy and action plan, the legal framework, the issue of socioeconomic and sociocultural factors, institutional and professional deficiencies, educational issues, lack of data and reporting, there is work to be done; there is also positive work being done, but there is a lot more to go." I heard.
Now, here, of course, he prepared a report on November 3, 2016, you are probably talking about that report. I looked at the report, we also worked with my friends, it is a pretty good report, really. I would like to thank the MPs before us. Its name is similar, there is only one difference: The Parliamentary Investigation Commission Established to Investigate All Kinds of Abuse Cases Against Children, Especially Sexual Abuse, and Determine the Precautions to be Taken. We sat down and worked with the government and the opposition, a complete report was prepared, but as a result, I do not think that we have improved since then, this is my opinion.

It is also possible to make long political analyzes here, but I do not think it is of any use. For example, in my opinion, in the country
There are resources, but it depends on where you allocate those resources. In my opinion, there is an expert - I am a well-established person on the other side of the table, I have a civil society background, I am a psychologist, etc. - there is also experience. Our issue should be about how we will implement this.
One: "There is no strategy or action plan." You said, Sir, what do you recommend about this, how should we start?
Two: We need to make a difference. I see that MPs serve here in good faith, regardless of their party. Sometimes we don't get along, we yell and scream, but people try to do something with good intentions, but it's not enough. How do we make the difference? For example, a brand new law for children? For example, we say positive things about GRASSES. One, it's not comprehensive enough; Secondly, it is an issue related only to the Ministry of Health. However, in Türkiye, a significant part of child services, even prevention-related work, is related to the Ministry of Family and Social Policies. Moreover, CIMs are an issue mostly related to sexual abuse, whereas abuse - as you explained, we learned more and refreshed our knowledge - has many different dimensions. Therefore, I would like to ask if there is a more comprehensive institutional proposal, is there an idea, is there anything that can be done or is there a way that should be discussed? Because I attribute the issue to the lack of a strategic action plan, the lack of capacity, especially the legal framework, institutional structure and personnel, and the lack of policies in the field of prevention, protection and strengthening. As experts around here - I sometimes find myself like that, after talking on that side for years and then coming here - where should we start, how should we do it? Because we intend to get results from this Commission, both the ruling party deputies and the opposition deputies here. I would like to ask what you would tell us as experts.
Best regards

records.title: Profesör Doktor Betül Ulukol ve Doçent Doktor Nilüfer Koçtürk’ün yaptıkları sunumlara ilişkin görüşme

fields.Subject: Profesör Doktor Betül Ulukol ve Doçent Doktor Nilüfer Koçtürk’ün yaptıkları sunumlara ilişkin görüşme

records.originalTurkish:
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Kısaca bir iki şey söylemek istiyorum. Hocam dedi ki: "Çok sayıda soru var ama bunlara yanıt veremiyorum." O zaman bir şeyi eksik bırakıyoruz demektir. Hoca sunum yapıyor... Eleştirmek için söylemiyorum ama eleştiriyorum. Şimdi, yani hocaya biz soru soruyorsak, hocada sorular varsa cevap vermiyorsa burada eksik bir şey var. Yani bu oturumlar iki üç saatte bitecek oturumlar değil. Ben sizi eleştirmiyorum, usulen, bize söylüyorum bunu. Yani o zaman niye çağırdık hocayı? Yani şimdi diyor ki: "Sorular var, sorulara cevap veremiyorum." Biz de diyoruz ki: "Ha, evet." O zaman biz boşa sorduk yani siyaseten konuştuk. Şimdi, bence buna bakmalıyız Sayın Başkanım yani birinci söyleyeceğim bu.
İkinci söyleyeceğim şey; burada görevli kamu görevlileri var, onları gördüm. Onların bize tanıtılmasını yani kimdir, nerede görevlidir, onları bilmemiz çok iyi olur. Biz de kimden nasıl yararlanabileceğimizi biliriz. İkinci ricam da bu.
Üçüncüsü de kaygımı söylüyorum: Bu ritimle gidersek biz bir şey öğrenemeyiz çünkü "İki üç saat toplanalım, haftada bir toplanalım." dersek zaten üç ay artı bir zamanımız var, toplamda işte 12-13 seans demektir bu, oradan bir şey olmaz. Yani o yüzden ritmimizi de bence kontrol etmemiz lazım. O kadar seanstan bir telepatik süreç olmaz yani. Değil mi Hocam? Dolayısıyla konuşmamız gereken şeyler var diye düşünüyorum, bunu da riyasetinize sunmak istedim.
records.englishTranslation:
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - I would like to say a few things briefly. My teacher said: "There are many questions, but I cannot answer them." Then we are missing something. The teacher is making a presentation... I'm not saying this to criticize, but I am criticizing. Now, if we ask questions to the teacher and the teacher has questions and does not answer, there is something missing here. So these sessions are not sessions that will end in two or three hours. I'm not criticizing you, I'm telling you this in a formal way. So why did we call the teacher then? So now he says: "There are questions, I cannot answer the questions." And we say: "Oh, yes." At that time, we asked in vain, that is, we talked politically. Now, I think we should look at this, Mr. President, so this is the first thing I will say.
The second thing I will say is; There are public officials on duty here, I saw them. It would be very good if we could introduce them to us, that is, who they are and where they are on duty. We know who we can benefit from and how. This is my second request.
Thirdly, I express my concern: If we continue with this rhythm, we will not learn anything because "Let's meet for two or three hours, let's meet once a week." If we say that we already have three months plus a period of time, this means 12-13 sessions in total, nothing will happen from there. That's why I think we need to control our rhythm. So there is no telepathic process from that many sessions. Isn't it right, Master? Therefore, I think there are things we need to talk about, and I wanted to present this to your presidency.
records.englishTitle: Interview regarding the presentations made by Professor Doctor Betül Ulukol and Associate Professor Nilüfer Koçtürk

englishFields.Subject: Interview regarding the presentations made by Professor Doctor Betül Ulukol and Associate Professor Nilüfer Koçtürk

records.title: Komisyonun çalışma programıyla ilgili önerilere, Komisyon üyelerinden kurulu bir heyet tarafından takip edilen davalara, Komisyonun çalışma gün ve saatlerine ilişkin görüşme münasebetiyle

fields.Subject: Komisyonun çalışma programıyla ilgili önerilere, Komisyon üyelerinden kurulu bir heyet tarafından takip edilen davalara, Komisyonun çalışma gün ve saatlerine ilişkin görüşme münasebetiyle

records.originalTurkish:
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Efendim, kısa bir bilgilendirme yapmak için söz istemiştim. Herkesi selamlıyorum.
Geçtiğimiz haftalarda gerek Sıla bebek için gerekse Narin'in davasına gidilmesi konusunda Meclisimizde bir görüş oluşmuştu. Ardından, Adalet Bakanlığımız aracılığıyla da mahkemeyle bir yazışma yapılmıştı. Sıla Bebek için de mahkemenin görüşü, suçluların 18 yaşından küçük olması nedeniyle bunun kapalı bir oturum olacağı ve vekillerin giremeyeceği yönünde olmuştu. Adalet Bakanlığından Sorumlu Genel Başkan Yardımcımız Gökçe Gökçen'in başkanlığında, 6 kişilik bir milletvekili heyetiyle biz Tekirdağ gittik Sayın Başkanım. Ben de davaya girdim -mahkeme bir ara karar verdi- çünkü bu Komisyon, bunun için kurulmuş bir komisyon; özellikle bu iki acı olay üzerine, yaşanan tartışmalar üzerine kurulmuştu. Mahkeme heyeti sağ olsun, bu hassasiyeti gösterdi. Ben de şu hassasiyeti gösterdim: Tutuklu çocukların ifadesi alınırken ben de salonda bulunmadım. Dolayısıyla, böyle bir ara yol bulunmuş oldu.
BAŞKAN CENGİZ AYDOĞDU - Orta çözüm olmuş, evet.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Evet, bir çözüm olmuş oldu. Mahkeme böyle bir ara karar verdi. Komisyonun bunu bilmesinin önemli olduğunu düşünüyorum.
Biz, Cumhuriyet Halk Partisi olarak bir sonraki davaya da tekrar katılıyor olacağız. Komisyon olarak gelmek isteyen arkadaşlarımız olursa onun da faydalı olacağını düşünüyorum çünkü bunun yasama faaliyeti açısından, milletvekillerinin görevleri açısından önemli bir konu olduğunu düşünüyorum. Yani bu konulara dair bir araştırma komisyonu kuruluyor ama o komisyon ilgili davalara giremiyorsa burada bir problem var. O yüzden, hani bu teknik tarafı da tek başına kıymetli bence. O nedenle de bu bilgiyi Komisyonla paylaşmak istedim.
records.englishTranslation:
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Sir, I asked for a brief briefing. I greet everyone.
In the past weeks, an opinion has formed in our Parliament about going to the case of both baby Sıla and Narin. Subsequently, a correspondence was made with the court through our Ministry of Justice. The court's opinion for Sıla Bebek was that since the criminals were under 18 years of age, this would be a closed session and deputies would not be able to enter. Mr. President, we went to Tekirdağ with a delegation of 6 members of parliament, under the chairmanship of our Deputy Chairman in Charge of the Ministry of Justice, Gökçe Gökçen. So I took part in the case - the court made an interim decision - because this Commission was established for this purpose; It was based on the discussions that took place, especially on these two painful events. Thanks to the court panel, it showed this sensitivity. I showed the following sensitivity: I was not present in the hall when the statements of the detained children were being taken. Therefore, such a middle way was found.
MAYOR CENGİZ AYDOĞDU - It is a middle solution, yes.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Yes, a solution has been found. The court made such an interim decision. I think it is important for the commission to know this.
We, as the Republican People's Party, will be participating in the next case again. If we have friends who want to come as a commission, I think it will also be useful because I think this is an important issue in terms of legislative activity and the duties of MPs. In other words, if an investigation commission is established on these issues, but that commission cannot deal with relevant cases, there is a problem here. That's why I think this technical aspect alone is valuable. That's why I wanted to share this information with the Commission.
records.englishTitle: On the occasion of the meeting regarding the proposals regarding the Commission's work program, the cases followed by a delegation of Commission members, and the Commission's working days and hours.

records.title: Komisyonun çalışma programıyla ilgili önerilere, Komisyon üyelerinden kurulu bir heyet tarafından takip edilen davalara, Komisyonun çalışma gün ve saatlerine ilişkin görüşme

fields.Subject: Komisyonun çalışma programıyla ilgili önerilere, Komisyon üyelerinden kurulu bir heyet tarafından takip edilen davalara, Komisyonun çalışma gün ve saatlerine ilişkin görüşme

records.originalTurkish:
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Efendim, kısa bir bilgilendirme yapmak için söz istemiştim. Herkesi selamlıyorum.
Geçtiğimiz haftalarda gerek Sıla bebek için gerekse Narin'in davasına gidilmesi konusunda Meclisimizde bir görüş oluşmuştu. Ardından, Adalet Bakanlığımız aracılığıyla da mahkemeyle bir yazışma yapılmıştı. Sıla Bebek için de mahkemenin görüşü, suçluların 18 yaşından küçük olması nedeniyle bunun kapalı bir oturum olacağı ve vekillerin giremeyeceği yönünde olmuştu. Adalet Bakanlığından Sorumlu Genel Başkan Yardımcımız Gökçe Gökçen'in başkanlığında, 6 kişilik bir milletvekili heyetiyle biz Tekirdağ gittik Sayın Başkanım. Ben de davaya girdim -mahkeme bir ara karar verdi- çünkü bu Komisyon, bunun için kurulmuş bir komisyon; özellikle bu iki acı olay üzerine, yaşanan tartışmalar üzerine kurulmuştu. Mahkeme heyeti sağ olsun, bu hassasiyeti gösterdi. Ben de şu hassasiyeti gösterdim: Tutuklu çocukların ifadesi alınırken ben de salonda bulunmadım. Dolayısıyla, böyle bir ara yol bulunmuş oldu.
BAŞKAN CENGİZ AYDOĞDU - Orta çözüm olmuş, evet.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (İstanbul) - Evet, bir çözüm olmuş oldu. Mahkeme böyle bir ara karar verdi. Komisyonun bunu bilmesinin önemli olduğunu düşünüyorum.
Biz, Cumhuriyet Halk Partisi olarak bir sonraki davaya da tekrar katılıyor olacağız. Komisyon olarak gelmek isteyen arkadaşlarımız olursa onun da faydalı olacağını düşünüyorum çünkü bunun yasama faaliyeti açısından, milletvekillerinin görevleri açısından önemli bir konu olduğunu düşünüyorum. Yani bu konulara dair bir araştırma komisyonu kuruluyor ama o komisyon ilgili davalara giremiyorsa burada bir problem var. O yüzden, hani bu teknik tarafı da tek başına kıymetli bence. O nedenle de bu bilgiyi Komisyonla paylaşmak istedim.
records.englishTranslation:
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Sir, I asked for a brief briefing. I greet everyone.
In the past weeks, an opinion has formed in our Parliament about going to the case of both baby Sıla and Narin. Subsequently, a correspondence was made with the court through our Ministry of Justice. The court's opinion for Sıla Bebek was that since the criminals were under 18 years of age, this would be a closed session and deputies would not be able to enter. Mr. President, we went to Tekirdağ with a delegation of 6 members of parliament, under the chairmanship of our Deputy Chairman in Charge of the Ministry of Justice, Gökçe Gökçen. So I took part in the case - the court made an interim decision - because this Commission was established for this purpose; It was based on the discussions that took place, especially on these two painful events. Thanks to the court panel, it showed this sensitivity. I showed the following sensitivity: I was not present in the hall when the statements of the detained children were being taken. Therefore, such a middle way was found.
MAYOR CENGİZ AYDOĞDU - It is a middle solution, yes.
SUAT ÖZÇAĞDAŞ (Istanbul) - Yes, a solution has been found. The court made such an interim decision. I think it is important for the commission to know this.
We, as the Republican People's Party, will be participating in the next case again. If we have friends who want to come as a commission, I think it will also be useful because I think this is an important issue in terms of legislative activity and the duties of MPs. In other words, if an investigation commission is established on these issues, but that commission cannot deal with relevant cases, there is a problem here. That's why I think this technical aspect alone is valuable. That's why I wanted to share this information with the Commission.
